## Outline (read this first)

**🚫** Still **no source edits** — read the tree for facts; change **only** this plan unless implementation is explicitly requested (see banner under **Status**).

**💩** Narrative and structure below
- Assistant draft except where a line is only ⚠️ / 👎 / ℹ️ tree fact
- If the fact is from code, framing around it may still need **💩** review

**✅** Contract summary — **✅** implemented in repository for items marked **✅** elsewhere (§ Concrete matches tree where ticked).

### `MarkdownGtk.Render` — **`public bool is_streaming`** (set **after** construction, not ctor arg)

- **✅** **`Render(Gtk.Box box)`** stays **single-argument** — **no** **`is_streaming`** in the **`Render`** ctor. Feed mode is **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; }`** on **`Render`**, set **after** **`new MarkdownGtk.Render`** by **`ChatView`** (§ Concrete **§ 1a.2**, **1a.3**). **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render` inside a fence — **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** only (§ **State flags** — content routing implies widgets; § **Proposed behaviour**, § Concrete **1c**) — **not** the same assignment path as the outer **`Render`**.
- **✅** **`ChatView`** / tests assign **`renderer.is_streaming`** when the **feed mode** changes between assistant messages (history replay vs live stream on the **same** long-lived `Render`) — not from a GObject **`default =`** on the property (§ Concrete).
- **✅** **Semantics** — **`is_streaming`** is a **behaviour / feed-mode marker** (non-streaming is the usual “bulk” shape, streaming is incremental feed). It is **not** “widget state” to wipe when clearing the view.
- **✅** **`Render.on_code_block(true, …)`** reads **`this.is_streaming`** and passes it into **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** — **at fence creation** (§ below).
- **ℹ️** **`Render.clear()` / `is_streaming`** — **Out of scope.** Opening a **new session** destroys and recreates the UI; a fresh **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** is constructed and **`is_streaming`** is set again at the next message boundary (**§ 1a.2** / **1a.3**). No plan requirement to touch **`clear()`** for this property.

### How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode (fundamental)

**✅** **Single rule — read once.** **`RenderSourceView` has no GObject `is_streaming` property**, no **`streaming` property**, and no **`Message` / `Message.is_stream`.** Those belong on **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** / **`Message`** only. **`new RenderSourceView(..., this.is_streaming)`** passes the value as the third ctor parameter **`bool is_streaming`** — **local to the ctor body only** (same value as **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open). **Do not** assign it to **`this.`** (no instance field). Ongoing routing uses **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**).

This was easy to bury under `Message.is_stream` prose; the rest belongs **here** in plain terms.

- **ℹ️** RSV **never** reads `OLLMchat.Message` or **`Message.is_stream`** — `libocmarkdowngtk` stays `OLLMchat`-free.
- **✅** **Mechanism:** the **only** `new RenderSourceView` site is **`Render.on_code_block`** (tree). **`Render`** passes **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** — third arg is ctor-local **`bool is_streaming`** (use inside the ctor only; **not** `this.is_streaming`, **not** a property). Later behaviour: **`has_widgets` / `has_content`**.
- **✅** **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open → that value is the ctor **`is_streaming`** argument for **ctor-time** setup only.
- **ℹ️** **`Message.is_stream`** matters **above** `Render` only (**`ChatView`** → **`renderer.is_streaming`**).

- **ℹ️** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box)`** then **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = has_content`** (§ **State flags** — `has_content` already implies embedded widgets for that routing). § Concrete **1c**. **Not** **`this.renderer.is_streaming`** — ctor-local **`is_streaming`** on RSV is **not** for nested routing; nested follows **`has_*`** only.

- **Problem**
  - **ℹ️** History / restore replay large assistant bodies through `MarkdownGtk.Render` → `RenderSourceView`.
  - **✅** **Addressed —** frame **may** defer GtkSource + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` to **`body_widgets()`** when policy allows.
  - **✅** **Addressed —** `add_code_text` skips scroll / newline resize when **`body_revealer.reveal_child`** is false; **`!has_content`** accumulates **`code_text`** off-tree.
  - **ℹ️** **Can the user collapse the frame body while the fence is still streaming?** — **Yes** when the header chevron exists. In the tree, **`collapse_toggle_button.visible`** is **`has_collapsed_style || this.collapse_on_done`** (`libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala`), so **`.collapsed-on-done`** thinking frames show the chevron **during** the stream — **not** only after **`end_code_block`** folds the body. **Automatic** fold at fence end (**`collapse_on_done`** in **`end_code_block`**) is separate from **manual** hide/show via that button.
    - **Data / routing:** **`has_content`** does **not** depend on **`body_revealer.reveal_child`**. While **`reveal_child`** is **false**, **`add_code_text`** still updates GtkSource / nested **`MarkdownGtk.Render.add`** when **`has_content`** (streaming path skips only scroll / newline resize when hidden — same idea as today ~597–600).
    - **Lazy embed — N/A** on incremental streaming rows: embed is built before first chunk (**`body_widgets()`** in ctor or immediate — § **Proposed behaviour**).

- **How fences get created (fundamental)**
  - **✅** `MarkdownGtk.Render` consumes markdown; when the **parser** sees an opening fence during `add()`, it calls `on_code_block(true, lang)` and **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** runs there — third ctor arg **`is_streaming`** equals **`Render.is_streaming`** at that instant (**ctor-local** only; § Outline **`Render.is_streaming`**).
  - **✅** **Live streaming feed** — set **`Render.is_streaming = true`** before **`add()`** for that message (typically via **`ChatView`** from **`Message.is_stream`**).
  - **✅** **History / load / restore / complete-message append** — set **`Render.is_streaming = false`** before **`add()`** for that message so lazy policy applies for bulk-shaped feeds.
  - **✅** **Implication:** **Do not** plumb `Message` into RSV — **`ChatView`** sets **`renderer.is_streaming`**; **`Render`** passes **`this.is_streaming`** into the RSV ctor at allocation (§ User requirements).

- **Fence delivery** (`ChatView` / `Render` only — § Wiring)
  - **ℹ️** `Message.is_stream` — on **`Message`**; **`ChatView`** maps it to **`renderer.is_streaming`** before **`add()`** for that assistant message.
  - **ℹ️** Stays on `Message` — RSV does not reference `Message`.
  - **ℹ️** **`Render.is_streaming`** → **`new RenderSourceView`** third parameter **`is_streaming`** (ctor-local) — § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**.
  - **ℹ️** Incremental at this fence — many `add_code_text` before `end_code_block`.
  - **ℹ️** Single-chunk / history / restore — one shot or replay — even if the message was streamed elsewhere.

- **Bulk / single-chunk fence**
  - **✅** Accumulate stored body with each `add_code_text` (today `code_content` shape — § Close in § Proposed behaviour).

- **Incremental fence** (many `add_code_text`)
  - **✅** Render types — `.collapsed-on-done` and Open only — `.collapsed` not used for this path (§ Proposed behaviour).
  - **✅** While fence open — buffers hold live text when `has_widgets` — finalize `code_text` at `end_code_block` — § Proposed behaviour.
  - **👎** No unconditional `+=` into stored body on every `add_code_text` (§ 👎).

- **State flags** (`RenderSourceView` — § Proposed behaviour)
  - **✅** **Authoritative.** The **only** approved **`RenderSourceView` state flags** are **`has_widgets`** and **`has_content`**. Do **not** add another state flag, rename these into a larger “flag set,” or suggest extra `has_*` / parallel booleans for the same roles. Ctor-local **`is_streaming`** (parameter), fence chrome, `code_text`, and similar are **not** state flags here — they are covered elsewhere (§ **Lazy body**, § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).
  - `has_widgets`
    - Embedded body exists (GtkSource + stack + nested markdown when applicable)
    - Set `true` only when `body_widgets()` has finished
  - `has_content`
    - Policy flag
    - When `true`, incoming `add_code_text` writes into GtkSource / nested render (requires `has_widgets`)
    - When `false`, incoming does not route to widgets
    - Not buffer length
    - Not empty vs non-empty
  - **ℹ️** Which property for which judgment — two roles, not interchangeable:
    - `has_widgets = false`
      - No embedded body yet (structural)
      - Indicator for “defer `body_widgets()`” / “nothing to append into GtkSource yet”
      - Not `source_view == null` alone (§ Acceptance)
      - Handlers: `!has_widgets` → may need `body_widgets()` first where policy allows (§ Lazy body)
    - `has_content`
      - Routing only
      - Whether incoming `add_code_text` should target widgets vs accumulate elsewhere
      - Example: `+=` stored body when buffers off-tree
      - Does not mean “embed exists”
      - Buffer writes still require `has_widgets`
      - Often one `add_code_text` while `has_widgets` false
      - Defer `body_widgets()` until policy is known
      - Then `has_content` tells whether chunk went to stored string vs buffers after embed exists (§ Proposed behaviour Non-streaming, § Concrete)
    - **ℹ️** `end_code_block` when `has_content` false for all incoming
      - Nothing routed into GtkSource / nested
      - Close does not finalize or clear those buffers (no-op there)
      - `code_text` / notify from stored body only (§ Proposed behaviour, § Concrete)
  - **ℹ️** Streaming → Open — at `end_code_block`, `has_content` stays `true` if content remains in widgets (body not cleared) — § Proposed behaviour.
  - **ℹ️** Does not flip `false` only because the fence ended.
  - **ℹ️** Non-streaming — `.collapsed`, `.collapsed-on-done`, open.
  - **ℹ️** Streaming — `.collapsed` N/A (§ Proposed behaviour).
  - **✅** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming`** — set from **`has_content`** once when the nested **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** is created in **`body_widgets()`** (**§ 1c**, § **5a**). **No** later updates — **`has_content`** does not change in a way that requires resyncing the nested renderer mid-fence for this plan.

- **Lazy body** (deferred GtkSource + nested markdown embed)
  - **✅** Ctor parses the fence and sets `this.` state needed later (including which render type).
  - **✅** **State flags (§ above)** — **`has_widgets`**, **`has_content`** only. **Other** instance members (e.g. `code_text`, fence chrome flags — `.collapsed`, `.collapsed-on-done` — exact names § Concrete) are **not** additional state flags. **`Render.is_streaming`** is passed only as ctor-local **`is_streaming`** — **not** stored on RSV. **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming` ← **`has_content`** (§ **State flags** bullet on nested **`Render`**, § **1c**).
  - **✅** No **`is_streaming` / `streaming` on RSV** — § **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (single rule).
  - **✅** Two new private methods — `body_widgets(bool has_content)`, `fill_widgets()` — **`fill_widgets()`** has no parameters.
    - **`body_widgets(bool has_content)`** — first real build: assigns **`this.has_content`** from the argument (after the **`has_widgets`** idempotency return), then creates GtkSource + stack + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` + revealer body wiring; sets `has_widgets = true` when done; does **not** push accumulated fence text into buffers or nested parser — § Proposed behaviour. **Does not** create **`view_source_toggle`** — that stays in the ctor (§ Concrete **4**). Call sites pass the routing policy (e.g. ctor: **`is_streaming || !this.collapse_on_done`**; lazy expand: **`true`**).
    - **`fill_widgets()`** — runs **after** `has_widgets`; copies `this.code_text` (and nested path as needed) into `source_buffer` / nested `MarkdownGtk.Render.add`, then triggers sizing / idle resize / **`full_view_toggle`** visibility so **`collapse_toggle_button`** / **`full_view_toggle`** paths and **`resize_widget_callback`** (**§ 6a.7**) see real content — § Concrete **5b**, § **6**.
    - **`body_widgets(bool has_content)`** does not resize for deferred content — **`fill_widgets()`** owns that “post-build” layout pass.
  - **✅** Handlers — `!has_widgets` → `body_widgets(…); fill_widgets()` when deferred `code_text` must appear in widgets (lazy / bulk off-tree); eager ctor passes routing into **`body_widgets(bool)`** — § Proposed behaviour per render type.
  - **ℹ️** Header / frame / revealer vs embedded bodies — § Concrete.

- **Frame chrome naming — two toggles (confusing today)** (`libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala`)
  - **✅** **`collapse_toggle_button`** — **Expand / collapse the frame body** for `.collapsed` / `.collapsed-on-done` frames (revealer show/hide). This is the **main** “is the body visible?” control; keep **Expand** / **Collapse** wording here.
  - **✅** **`expand_button`** (historical) → **`full_view_toggle`** — **Short vs full height** of the **code body** inside the frame (clamped natural height vs taller — the pan-down / pan-up control). It is **not** the same as collapsing the frame; it only changes how much of the **already-visible** body you see.
  - **ℹ️** **Rename in implementation** — field / variable: **`expand_button`** → **`full_view_toggle`**; local **`is_expanded`** (or equivalent) → e.g. **`full_view_open`** — § Concrete **3b**, **4b**, **6a.7**. Update tooltips/icons copy if needed so “full view” vs “frame collapse” stay distinct.
  - **ℹ️** **Collapsed frame body — which handlers touch widgets / `fill_widgets()`** — For **`.collapsed`** chrome, **`copy_button`**, **`view_source_toggle`**, and **`full_view_toggle`** are **not for** **`body_widgets()` / `fill_widgets()`** in their **`clicked`** handlers: they are **hidden until** the body is expanded (tree), and collapse **hides** them again — the **expand** path (**`collapse_toggle_button`**) already ran **`body_widgets(true); fill_widgets()`** on lazy policy. **`clicked`** here is **only** clipboard / stack flip / resize — **§6a.2**, **§6a.3**, **§4** **`view_source_toggle`**. If any of those controls stay **visible** while the frame body is collapsed, **that is a bug**.
  - **ℹ️** **`new_chat_button`** — **`visible = is_user_frame`** (tree — **`oc-frame-user`**); **not** shown on typical **`.collapsed`** assistant fences today (could be added later). **`start_new_chat_requested`** must use **`this.code_text`** (§ **Concrete** **§3**) — **not** **`GtkSource.Buffer`** + **`TextIter`** (today’s tree). **✅** **Plan contract:** **`code_text`** is the **authoritative** fence-body string for this block; **`add_code_text`** / **`end_code_block`** (§ **Proposed behaviour**) keep **`code_text`** accurate whenever **`start_new_chat_requested`** can run, including while **`!has_widgets`** (bulk / lazy **`code_text`** only) — same string **`fill_widgets()`** pushes into GtkSource. The **`new_chat`** handler **does not** need **`body_widgets()`** / **`fill_widgets()`** just to read text.

- **Layering**
  - **ℹ️** `Message.is_stream` stays on `OLLMchat.Message` — **`ChatView`** reads it — **`renderer.is_streaming =`** … **after** **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(box)`** (plain `bool`, no `OLLMchat` types in `libocmarkdowngtk`).
  - **✅** `RenderSourceView` — **state flags:** `has_widgets`, `has_content` only (§ **State flags**). **Elsewhere:** fence chrome; ctor-local **`is_streaming`** from **`Render`** at **`new RenderSourceView`** (§ **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** — **not** a state flag, **not** an instance field). **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render.is_streaming` — set **once** from **`has_content`** in **`body_widgets()`** (**§ 1c**, § **5a**).
  - **✅** **👎** Public **`is_stream`** / **`is_streaming`** / **`streaming`** on **`RenderSourceView`** — rejected; constraint **✅** satisfied in tree (§ **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).

## User requirements

**✅** Authoritative — items below **✅** are implemented in tree unless noted.

- **✅** **Process — mandatory** — **Do not edit repository code** in service of this plan *(drafting rule; **✅** implementation subsequently authorized and landed for items marked in this file).*
- **✅** **Accepted** — **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; }`** (no **`default =`**). Set **after** **`new`** by **`ChatView`** (**§ 1a.2**, **1a.3**). **Nested** `Render` (markdown-in-fence): **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** in **`RenderSourceView`** — § **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**, § **1c** — no `OLLMchat` in `libocmarkdowngtk`.
- **✅** **Accepted** — **`RenderSourceView`** — third ctor parameter **`bool is_streaming`** — **local to the ctor**; **not** a property, **not** `this.is_streaming` or any instance field. **`Render`** passes **`this.is_streaming`** at **`new RenderSourceView`**. Ongoing policy: **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**; § Concrete.
- **✅** **Rejected** — any **`is_streaming`**, **`streaming`**, or **`is_stream`** **property** or **instance field** on **`RenderSourceView`** — use **`has_widgets`** / **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**) and fence chrome (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**). *(Constraint **✅** satisfied in tree.)*
- **✅** **Accepted** — **state flags:** `has_widgets`, `has_content` **only** (§ **State flags**). **Also:** `code_text`, fence chrome flags — § Outline — exact members § Concrete (not state flags).
- **✅** `OLLMchat.Message.is_stream` — message-level hint — `ChatView` / `Render` only (§ Wiring).
- **✅** Lazy path — omit embedded body creation in ctor when policy requires — `body_widgets()` on first need; `fill_widgets()` when accumulated `code_text` must be applied into the new embed (§ Outline **Lazy body**).

## 👎 Do not do / rejected

- **👎** **Touching the codebase without a direct user order** — **never** edit `Render.vala`, `RenderSourceView.vala`, `ChatView.vala`, or related sources while drafting or revising this plan. § Concrete describes **future** edits; it is **not** permission to apply them.
- **👎** Internal “pending” buffer inside `RenderSourceView` — no second `string` / `StringBuilder` / queue for chunks when `code_text` is deferred — § Outline streaming rules instead.
- **👎** Standing reconcile in `end_code_block` when not streaming — unless a real bug proves divergence (then fix cause).
- **👎** Optional product — first expand = source only — not in scope unless product asks.
- **👎** A **second** obscure stream flag on **`Render`** alongside **`is_streaming`** (e.g. duplicate “hint” APIs) — one **well-named** public **`bool`** only (§ Outline).
- **👎** Duplicating “**no streaming / `is_streaming` API on RSV**” in new bullets — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** is canonical; do not pad the plan with repeats.
- **👎** Extra methods on `RenderSourceView` beyond `body_widgets(bool has_content)` and `fill_widgets()` — not without updating this plan.
- **👎** **`fill_widgets()`** before **`body_widgets(…)`** (or when `has_widgets` is false) — structure first, then content.
- **👎** Any **third** (or further) **state flag** on **`RenderSourceView`** beyond **`has_widgets`** and **`has_content`** — not approved (§ Proposed behaviour **State flags**).
- **👎** `MarkdownGtk.Render` owning `Message` — `Message.is_stream` stays in `libollmchat`.
- **👎** `accumulate_code_text_per_add` (and similar unreviewed flags) — not approved.
- **✅** **`add_code_text`** — **`this.code_text += text`** only when **`!has_content`** (off-tree / lazy accumulation). When **`has_content`** is **true**, chunks go to GtkSource / nested only; **`code_text`** is reconciled **at `end_code_block`** from the buffer when **`has_widgets`** — § **6.1** / **§ 6.2**. **No** **`renderer.is_streaming`** (or any **`Render`** read) inside **`RenderSourceView`** feed methods — **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** only.
- **👎** Extra **`Render` / `ChatView` surface** beyond **`Render.is_streaming`** + message-boundary wiring in § Concrete — keep fence delivery minimal (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **👎** Streaming (incremental fence) + `.collapsed` render type — not in this plan — not used (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **👎** **New width / allocation guards** — do **not** add **`get_allocated_width`**, **`for_width`**, or “zero width” branches for lazy attach; keep **existing** `resize_widget_callback` / tree behaviour only.

## Existing indicators (**ℹ️** — tree)

- **ℹ️** `OLLMchat.Message.is_stream` — `true` for `content-stream` and `think-stream`; `false` for `content-non-stream` (`libollmchat/Message.vala`).
- **ℹ️** `ChatView` — live: `append_assistant_chunk`; history: `append_complete_assistant_message` (~559).
- **ℹ️** `MarkdownGtk.Render` — incremental `add` (`libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`).

---

## Placeholder vs empty `body_revealer` (**ℹ️**, **💩**)

- **ℹ️** `Gtk.Revealer.set_child` may be `null` (GTK docs).
- **ℹ️** Lazy option — no body child until `body_widgets()` / attach — then one `set_child` for the real scrolled body.
- **ℹ️** Today — `body_revealer.set_child(this.scrolled_window)` in ctor (~378–386).
- **👎** **No new allocation / width guards** — do **not** add defensive **`get_allocated_width`**, **`for_width <= 0`**, or similar checks beyond what the **existing tree** already does (**§ 👎**).
- **👎** Placeholder widget only to replace later — prefer null or defer attach (§ Outline).

---

## Purpose (**💩**)

- **✅** Reduce work during history load and restore for large fenced blocks.
- **✅** Defer creating GtkSource + nested markdown embed until needed (§ Outline, `body_widgets()`).
- **✅** Lifecycle — Streaming / Non-streaming × render types — `has_widgets` / `has_content` — § Proposed behaviour (streaming — no `.collapsed`).

---

## Scope (**💩** — table draft)

| In scope | Out of scope |
|----------|----------------|
| `RenderSourceView` — `has_widgets`, `has_content`, `code_text`, fence chrome flags, `body_widgets(bool has_content)`, `fill_widgets()`, handlers — **no** `is_streaming` / `streaming` API (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**) | New public methods without approval (`CODING_STANDARDS.md`) |
| `ChatView` / `Render` — `Message.is_stream`, incremental vs single-chunk delivery — § Wiring | Parser (6.8), ProgressView / Runner (7.14.2.2) |

## Acceptance criteria (**💩** mixed — each line below **✅** verified in tree)

**✅** All acceptance lines below verified in tree (subject to remaining matrix nuance in § Proposed behaviour).

- **✅** Lazy embed / restore — no nested `Parser.add` / GtkSource insert for the body until `body_widgets()`; deferred bulk into widgets via `fill_widgets()` (or agreed min — source only).
- **✅** Fence delivery — incremental vs single-chunk — from `ChatView` / `Render` / `Message.is_stream` — RSV feed mode § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (§ Wiring).
- **✅** Incremental fence — `add_code_text` does not append stored body while open — § Outline.
- **✅** Incremental fence — stored body set at close from SourceView (and nested path as needed) — § Outline.
- **✅** Single-chunk / bulk — stored body accumulates per `add_code_text` like today (`code_content`) — § Outline.
- **✅** `end_code_block` — snapshot / `code_text` / `has_content` per § Proposed behaviour — not user revealer toggle (out of scope here).
- **✅** `has_widgets`, `has_content` — explicit state — not `source_view == null` alone — `has_content` = route incoming to widgets, not buffer non-emptiness (§ Proposed behaviour).
- **✅** Handlers that need the body — ensure `body_widgets()` has run before touching GtkSource / nested render; if content lived in `code_text` only, `fill_widgets()` after `body_widgets()` — § Outline.

---

## Current behaviour (reference)

- **✅** **Superseded —** ctor **may** defer GtkSource + stack + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` to **`body_widgets()`** per policy (`libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala`).
- **✅** **Superseded —** `add_code_text` uses **`code_text`**, **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** routing — § **6.1** landed.
- **✅** Collapse hides body — buffers retained; revealer default hidden where policy defers paint (**✅** `reveal_child` / ctor follow-up).

---

## Wiring (**ℹ️** — `libocmarkdowngtk` stays `OLLMchat`-free)

- **✅** **Session restore / history replay** — **not** a streaming feed shape for this plan: set **`renderer.is_streaming = false`** before **`add()`** for restored or complete-message assistant bodies (**§ 1a.3**). **Live** chunks use **`Message.is_stream`** (**§ 1a.2**). Same long-lived **`Render`** may **flip** when switching replay → live (§ Outline **`Render.is_streaming`**).
- **ℹ️** RSV **fence** — ctor-local **`is_streaming`** from **`Render.is_streaming`** at **`on_code_block`** — § Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode** (parameter only; not stored).
- **ℹ️** **Nested** `MarkdownGtk.Render` — **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**, § **1c**).
- **ℹ️** `libocmarkdowngtk` must not depend on `OLLMchat`.
- **✅** `append_assistant_chunk` vs `append_complete_assistant_message` — different feed shapes; **`ChatView`** must set **`renderer.is_streaming`** accordingly **each** message (§ User requirements).
- **ℹ️** `RenderSourceView` — **`has_widgets`**, **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**), fence chrome, ctor-local **`is_streaming`** at **`new RenderSourceView`** (not stored), `add_code_text` / `end_code_block` — no `Message` on RSV (§ Outline **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**).

---

## Proposed behaviour (contract) (**💩** — whole section is assistant matrix unless you promoted it; cross-check § User requirements + 👎)

**✅** **Matrix scope:** **Streaming** = parser-driven fence / incremental `add_code_text` (§ Outline **How fences get created**). **Non-streaming** = bulk / history / restore where behaviour differs (**not** the same incremental fence discovery path for this plan’s targets). *(Definitions **✅** used in tree.)*

**💩** — **ℹ️** Matrix (assistant)
- Two axes: fence mode (Streaming vs Non-streaming) × render type
- Track `has_widgets` and `has_content` at ctor / incoming / close
- Close means `end_code_block` only
- Not user toggling revealer / expand / collapse (out of scope here)

**💩** — **ℹ️** Streaming + `.collapsed` (body hidden at start)
- **NOT SUPPORTED** — **NOT USED**
- Live incremental fences use `.collapsed-on-done` (`libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala`)
- Do not plan or implement streaming + `.collapsed`

**💩** — **ℹ️** Flag meanings
- `has_widgets` — embedded body built (`body_widgets()` finished)
- `has_content` — incoming stream should write to the widgets (buffers / nested render)
- Routing policy
- Not “is there text in the buffer yet”

**💩** — **ℹ️** `code_text`
- Persisted string
- Updated at `end_code_block` / fence finished (§ Concrete)

**💩** — **ℹ️** Single-chunk fence
- Treat as Non-streaming in this matrix even if message streams (§ Outline)

- **Streaming** (incremental `add_code_text` — `.collapsed-on-done` or Open)
  - **Render type — Collapsed** (`.collapsed`)
    - **NOT SUPPORTED** — **NOT USED** — no ctor / incoming / close matrix — `ChatView` thinking uses `collapsed-on-done`, not `.collapsed`.

  - **Render type — Collapse at end** (`.collapsed-on-done` — chrome name from tree; behaviour here only ctor / incoming / `end_code_block`)
    - **💩** **Ctor** — streaming collapse-at-end
      - **ℹ️** Assistant detail draft — confirm before build
      - **Parse**
        - Opening fence and attributes
        - Record collapse-at-end from chrome
        - Names: `.collapsed-on-done` / `collapse_on_done` (exact identifiers § Concrete)
        - Meaning: body hidden until fence closes (revealer / product)
        - Not the same as `has_widgets` false
      - `body_widgets()`
        - Call in ctor or immediately after parse
        - Required on this matrix row
        - GtkSource + stack + nested `MarkdownGtk.Render` before first `add_code_text`
        - Body may still be visually collapsed
        - On success: `has_widgets` = true
      - `has_content`
        - Set by routing policy, not by ctor completion alone
        - Usually `has_content` = true before first `add_code_text`
        - Chunks go to buffers / nested, not `+=` stored per chunk (§ 👎)
        - Flag means route incoming here
        - Does not mean buffer already non-empty
        - Does not mean revealer is expanded
      - **Guard**
        - If `body_widgets()` never runs, `has_widgets` stays false
        - Should not happen on this row
        - Handlers § Outline
    - **Incoming**
      - When `has_content`: `add_code_text` writes buffers / nested — `has_widgets` = true
      - Do not `+=` stored string per chunk on incremental path (§ 👎)
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - Finalize `code_text` from SourceView / nested (§ Concrete)
      - `has_content` = false
      - Fence ended
      - No further `add_code_text` for this block (stop routing incoming to widgets)
      - Clear buffers / flush nested per tree (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Open** (body visible from start — LLM content as it runs)
    - **Ctor**
      - `body_widgets()` in ctor or immediately after parse — `has_widgets` = true before first incoming
      - `has_content` = true before first incoming
      - Incoming stream will write into widgets (same routing flag as during the fence)
    - **Incoming**
      - `has_widgets` = true; `has_content` = true while `add_code_text` feeds the fence
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - Write `code_text` from buffers (§ Concrete)
      - Do not clear visible body
      - Content stays on screen
      - `has_content` = true (content still in widgets; not `false` just because the fence closed) — § Concrete

- **Non-streaming** (single-chunk, history, restore — `.collapsed` allowed)
  - **Render type — Collapsed**
    - **Ctor**
      - `has_widgets` = false, `has_content` = false — parse only (§ Outline)
    - **Incoming**
      - Often one `add_code_text`
      - `has_widgets` false → defer `body_widgets()` until policy is fixed
      - After `body_widgets()` (`has_widgets` true), `has_content` selects routing
      - `+=` stored body when policy keeps buffers off-tree `has_content` false (§ Concrete)
    - **Close** (`end_code_block`)
      - `code_text` matches accumulated incoming; notify (§ Outline)
      - If `has_content` was false throughout (nothing routed to widgets), widget finalize / clear are no-ops
      - Only stored string mattered (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Collapse at end**
    - **💩** **Ctor** — non-streaming collapse-at-end
      - **ℹ️** Assistant detail draft — confirm before build
      - **Parse**
        - Opening fence and attributes
        - Record collapse-at-end from chrome (`.collapsed-on-done`, § Concrete / tree)
        - Restore / history: block was hidden until done
        - Distinct from non-streaming `.collapsed` only (§ Proposed behaviour)
      - **Chrome without embed**
        - Frame, header, revealer as today when embed not required yet
        - § Placeholder vs `body_revealer`
      - **Lazy embed** (default when policy allows)
        - Do not call `body_widgets()` in ctor
        - After parse: `has_widgets` = false
        - After parse: `has_content` = false
        - No GtkSource / stack / nested markdown yet
        - Incoming cannot route into widgets that do not exist
        - Single-chunk may only `+=` stored body until `body_widgets()` (§ Outline)
      - **Eager embed** (policy requires embed before `add_code_text`)
        - Call `body_widgets()` in ctor or right after parse
        - On return: `has_widgets` = true
        - `has_content` still from policy alone
        - `has_content` = true only when incoming should write GtkSource / nested
        - `has_content` = false when incoming should only `+=` stored string
        - Example: buffers off-tree until expand or `end_code_block` (§ Concrete)
        - `body_widgets()` does not imply `has_content` = true (§ Outline)
      - **Revealer vs `has_widgets`**
        - Visually collapsed body does not imply `has_widgets` false
        - User may not see scrolled body yet
        - `has_widgets`: whether embed exists in widget tree
    - **Incoming**
      - `+=` stored body each `add_code_text`
      - Buffers when `has_widgets` and `has_content`
      - `has_content` — `true` when policy routes incoming into widgets (independent of how many characters so far)
    - **Close**
      - Stored body complete — notify
      - `has_content` = false at `end_code_block`
      - `code_text` holds snapshot (§ Concrete)

  - **Render type — Open**
    - **Ctor**
      - `body_widgets()` early — `has_widgets` = true
    - **Incoming**
      - `+=` stored body
      - Buffers in sync
    - **Close**
      - Notify
      - May persist `code_text` without clearing buffers (same idea as streaming Open)

- **Shared** (all modes / types)
  - `body_widgets(bool has_content)` — private — assigns **`this.has_content`** from the argument (after idempotency return), then builds embed and sets **`has_widgets` = true** — § Outline, § Concrete **5a**
  - `fill_widgets()` — private, no parameters — applies `code_text` into buffers / nested markdown, then layout/sizing so **`full_view_toggle`** / **`collapse_toggle_button`** paths and resize work — § Outline **Lazy body**, § Concrete **5b**
  - Handlers — `!has_widgets` where embed required — `body_widgets(…)` with the correct routing **`bool`**, then `fill_widgets()` when deferred string must become visible widget content (§ Outline)
  - **💩** — **ℹ️** `end_code_block`
    - Widget snapshot / clear steps apply only where content went through `has_content` routing into widgets
    - Otherwise close is stored-string / notify only (§ Outline state flags)

---

## Concrete code proposals

**🚫** Fenced Vala below is **spec only**. **Do not** apply patches to the repo from this section unless the user explicitly asks for implementation. Same as **Status** banner and § User requirements (process).

**ℹ️** Order
- Do one numbered block at a time
- Each block ends with Keep / Remove / Replace with / Add + fenced Vala per `docs/guide-to-writing-plans.md` before starting the next
- Avoids the last mixed diff

### 1. Call sites — `new MarkdownGtk.Render` (tree) — **✅**

**ℹ️** **`Render`** stays **`Render(Gtk.Box box)`** only — **no** second ctor argument (§ **`libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`** — **`Add`** **`is_streaming`** property only).

**✅** **Outer `Render` / fence** — **`Render.on_code_block` → `new RenderSourceView(..., this.is_streaming)`** reads **`Render.is_streaming` at fence open.** Set **`renderer.is_streaming`** **before `add()`** for that assistant message (**§ 1a.2**, **§ 1a.3**). **✅** **Nested `MarkdownGtk.Render`** — **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (§ **State flags**, § **Proposed behaviour**) **before** nested **`start()`** (**§ 1c**). If **`has_*` routing** does not match the matrix, **nothing in this plan’s matrix applies**.

**💩** **`public bool is_streaming { get; set; default = false; }`** — **Rejected** — redundant **`default =`**; value comes from **explicit** assignments (§ Outline).

### 1a. `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — assistant `Render` — **✅**

**ℹ️** **No** **`Remove` / `Replace with`** — tree already has **`new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.text_view_box) { … }`**. Feed mode is **§ 1a.2** / **1a.3** only.

### 1a.2 `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — **`initialize_assistant_message`** (live / streaming chunks) — **✅**

**Why:** First line that puts **`Message.is_stream`** into **`Render`** for the current assistant round — **before** any **`process_new_chunk` → `add()`**.

#### Keep (anchor — last lines before insert)

```vala
			this.content_state = ContentState.NONE;
			// Initialize the renderer for the new assistant message
			this.renderer.start();
```

#### Add

```vala
			this.renderer.is_streaming = response.message.is_stream;
```

### 1a.3 `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` — **`append_complete_assistant_message`** (history / complete body / **session restore**) — **✅**

**Why:** This method always feeds the markdown pipeline **one complete body at a time** (bulk-shaped) — **not** incremental fence delivery. That is true for **session restore** and for **live** `on_message_created` paths (`"ui"`, `"ui-warning"`, `"think-stream"`, `"content-stream"`, …) that call **`append_complete_assistant_message`** (`libollmchatgtk/ChatWidget.vala`). **`renderer.is_streaming`** must be **`false`** so lazy policy and **`code_text`** accumulation match that feed shape (**§ Proposed behaviour**). Do **not** copy **`message.is_stream`** here: roles such as **`content-stream`** / **`think-stream`** set **`Message.is_stream = true`**, but the **UI delivery** is still a single bulk append, not chunk-by-chunk streaming.

#### Keep

```vala
			this.is_thinking = is_thinking;
```

#### Add

```vala
			this.renderer.is_streaming = false;
```

*(Insert **Add** immediately after **Keep**; existing comments and **`// Process thinking …`** block follow unchanged.)*

### 1b. `examples/oc-test-gtkmd.vala` — demo `Render` (**optional** — test harness only) — **✅**

**Why:** Keeps the demo consistent when someone runs it; **not** part of product behaviour (**§ User requirements** for **`ChatView`** are **§ 1a.2** / **1a.3**).

#### Keep (anchor)

```vala
		this.md_renderer = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.text_view_box) {
			scroll_to_end = this.history_messages.get_length() == 0 && stream
		};
```

#### Add (immediately after **Keep**)

```vala
		this.md_renderer.is_streaming = stream;
```

### 1c. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — nested `Render` (markdown code blocks) — **✅**

**Why:** § **State flags** — set **`nested_markdown_render.is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** once when creating the nested **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** in **`body_widgets()`** — § **Proposed behaviour**.

#### Remove

```vala
				this.nested_markdown_render = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box);
				this.nested_markdown_render.start();
```

#### Replace with

```vala
				this.nested_markdown_render = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box);
				this.nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = this.has_content;
				this.nested_markdown_render.start();
```

**ℹ️** **`body_widgets(bool has_content)`** sets **`this.has_content`** before nested **`start()`** (§ **5a**).

---

### 2. Call sites — `new RenderSourceView` (tree) — **✅**

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala` | **`new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming)`** inside `on_code_block` — passes **Render** feed mode as ctor-local **`is_streaming`** (§ Outline **fundamental**) |
| `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` | Ctor **`(Render, string, bool is_streaming)`** — local parameter only (**not** property, **not** field) + behaviour |
| `libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala` | No `new RenderSourceView` — **`renderer.is_streaming`** from **`Message.is_stream`** in **`initialize_assistant_message`** only (**§ 1a.2**); **`append_complete_assistant_message`** sets **`false`** (bulk feed; **not** **`Message.is_stream`** — § **1a.3**); **`thinking_frame = renderer.childview`** after manual `on_code_block` for thinking |

**Ctor third arg** — **`bool is_streaming`** ← **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open (**ctor-local**; not stored on RSV) — **`Render.is_streaming`** must be set **before `add()`** for that message (§ **1** **✅**, § **1a.2**, **1a.3**).

---

### `libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala` — **✅**

**Why:** **`public bool is_streaming`** — assistant **`Render`:** set **after** **`new`** (**`ChatView`** — **§ 1a.2**, **§ 1a.3**), read at **`on_code_block`** for **`new RenderSourceView`**. **Nested** **`Render`:** **`is_streaming`** from **`has_content`** (**§ 1c**, § **State flags**).

#### Add (property — outline, no `default`)

```vala
public bool is_streaming { get; set; }
```

*(Docblock: set by app **after** construction, before **`add()`** for each assistant feed; read at **`on_code_block`**.)*

**ℹ️** If Vala insists on an initializer for the auto-property, use a **private backing field** — avoid **`default =`** on the public contract (implementation detail).

#### Remove

```vala
this.childview = new RenderSourceView(this, lang);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
this.childview = new RenderSourceView(this, lang, this.is_streaming);
```

**Why:** Third argument is ctor-local **`is_streaming`** (= **`Render.is_streaming`** at fence open); **not** stored on RSV.

---

### `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — **✅**

**Why:** Lazy / `has_widgets` / `has_content` / `body_widgets()` / `fill_widgets()` — § Concrete steps **3–6**; ctor gains third parameter **`bool is_streaming`** — **local variable in the ctor only**; **not** a GObject property, **not** `this.is_streaming` or any instance field (§ **How `RenderSourceView` gets feed mode**). **Not** a state flag — § **State flags**.

#### Add (ctor signature — outline)

```vala
public RenderSourceView(Render renderer, string language_id, bool is_streaming)
```

*(Use **`is_streaming`** only inside the ctor body where ctor-time setup needs it. **Do not** assign **`is_streaming`** to **`this.`** Later logic: **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** — § step 4 when approved.)*

### 3. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — state flags and `code_text` — **✅** *(§3 `has_collapsed_style` field — **✅** not used; ctor-local + `body_revealer.reveal_child` after `body_widgets()` instead.)*

#### Remove

```vala
		private string code_language = "";
		private StringBuilder code_content = new StringBuilder();
```

#### Replace with

```vala
		private string code_language = "";
		private string code_text = "";
		private bool has_widgets = false;
		private bool has_content = false;
		private bool has_collapsed_style = false;
```

**ℹ️** Ctor assigns **`this.has_collapsed_style`** from the same frame-theme parse as today’s local **`has_collapsed_style`** (then prefer **`this.has_collapsed_style`** in header/title margins so **`body_widgets()`** can read it).

### 3b. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — `container_box`; rename **`expand_button`** → **`full_view_toggle`** — **✅**

**Why:** **`body_widgets()`** must **`append`** **`body_revealer`** to the same vertical box as the header (**§ 5a**). **`full_view_toggle`** naming — § Outline **Frame chrome naming**, § **6a.3**, § **6a.7**.

#### Remove

```vala
		private Gtk.Button expand_button;
```

#### Replace with

```vala
		private Gtk.Button full_view_toggle;
		private Gtk.Box container_box;
```

#### Remove

```vala
			this.renderer = renderer;
			this.code_content = new StringBuilder();
```

#### Replace with

```vala
			this.renderer = renderer;
			
```

### 4. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — ctor — **✅**

**✅** Ctor still **parses**, builds **header / frame chrome**, **container**, **frame**, and **realize** hooks — same **overall** flow as `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` today. **Do not** relocate large blocks (for example the whole `header_box` tree).

**✅** **Remove** (from the ctor, into **`body_widgets()`** / **`fill_widgets()`** as appropriate) **only** the **creation** and **event wiring** of widgets that are **actually deferred** — **(1)** GtkSource **buffer + `GtkSource.View`** and **(2)** the **body embed**: outer `ScrolledWindow`, `stack`, `rendered_box`, inner source `ScrolledWindow`, markdown branch (`nested_markdown_render`), and **`body_revealer`** through `set_child(this.scrolled_window)`. Treating the **outer** `ScrolledWindow` as part of that embed is a **fair** minimal cut (same lazy boundary as the stack). **`has_widgets`** and **`has_content`** come from **`body_widgets(bool has_content)`** (ctor passes **`is_streaming || !this.collapse_on_done`** on the eager path).

**✅** **`view_source_toggle`** — **light touch:** **keep** **`view_source_toggle`** **creation** and its **`clicked`** handler **in the ctor** (same behaviour as today). **Do not** list them under **Remove** or move them into **`body_widgets()`**. Order: build the embed first (**`body_widgets()`** on the eager path, or when lazy policy first needs the body), **then** the existing toggle block so **`stack`** / **`source_view`** / **`rendered_box`** exist. Visibility lines that only set **`view_source_toggle.visible`** / **`copy_button.visible`** stay in the ctor with the toggle — see **Keep** below; they are **not** part of the embed **Remove** hunks.

**✅** The ctor still **`connect`s** signals on header buttons, **`view_source_toggle`**, **`full_view_toggle`** (rename from **`expand_button`** — § Outline **Frame chrome naming**; § **4b**), **`new_chat_button`**, and (when the embed exists) **`source_view`** / **`rendered_box`** realize — same sites as today. On the **lazy** path **`body_revealer`**, **`stack`**, **`source_view`**, etc. **do not exist** until **`body_widgets()`** — treat as **null / unusable** when **`!has_widgets`**. **`new_chat_button`** **`clicked`** uses **`this.code_text`** only (§ Outline **`new_chat_button`**, § **6a.4**) — **no** **`source_buffer`** read. After **`body_widgets()`** in a handler that needs the embed, **`fill_widgets()`** runs immediately (§ **6**).

**✅** **`this.container_box`** — holds **header** + **`body_revealer`** (revealer appended in **`body_widgets()`**, not left dangling). **`frame.set_child(this.container_box)`** stays in the ctor (**§ 4a**).

#### Remove

```vala
		public RenderSourceView(Render renderer, string language_id)
```

#### Replace with

```vala
		public RenderSourceView(Render renderer, string language_id, bool is_streaming)
```

### 4a. Ctor — `this.container_box`; `frame.set_child(this.container_box)` — **✅**

**Why:** **`body_widgets()`** appends **`body_revealer`** to **`this.container_box`** (**§ 5a**). The ctor must use **`this.container_box`**, not a **`var`**, so the lazy path can attach the body later.

#### Remove

```vala
			var container_box = new Gtk.Box(Gtk.Orientation.VERTICAL, 0) {
				hexpand = true,
				vexpand = false
			};
			
			// Add header box to container
			container_box.append(header_box);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
			this.container_box = new Gtk.Box(Gtk.Orientation.VERTICAL, 0) {
				hexpand = true,
				vexpand = false
			};
			
			// Add header box to container
			this.container_box.append(header_box);
```

#### Remove

```vala
			frame.set_child(container_box);
```

#### Replace with

```vala
			frame.set_child(this.container_box);
```

### 4b. Ctor — **`expand_button`** → **`full_view_toggle`** (pan-up / pan-down **body height**) — **✅**

**Why:** Same behaviour as today; **rename + tooltips** only (§ Outline **Frame chrome naming**). **§ 6a.3** duplicate **Replace with** — use **this** block or that one, not both.

#### Remove

```vala
			// Track expanded state for this code block
			bool is_expanded = false;
			
			// Create Expand/Collapse button with icon (created hidden, made visible by other code)
			var expand_button = new Gtk.Button() {
```

#### Replace with

```vala
			bool full_view_open = false;
			
			var full_view_toggle = new Gtk.Button() {
```

#### Remove

```vala
			// Store expand button reference
			this.expand_button = expand_button;

### 5a. `body_widgets(bool has_content)` — **✅**

#### Add

```vala
private void body_widgets(bool has_content)
{
	if (this.has_widgets) {
		return;
	}
	this.has_content = has_content;

	// GtkSource buffer + view (language = first fence token, already in this.code_language)
	GtkSource.Buffer source_buffer;
	if (this.code_language != "") {
		var mapped_id = this.map_language_id(this.code_language);
		var lang_manager = GtkSource.LanguageManager.get_default();
		var gtk_lang = lang_manager.get_language(mapped_id);
		if (gtk_lang != null) {
			source_buffer = new GtkSource.Buffer.with_language(gtk_lang);
		} else {
			source_buffer = new GtkSource.Buffer(null);
		}
	} else {
		source_buffer = new GtkSource.Buffer(null);
	}

	this.source_view = new GtkSource.View() {
		editable = false,
		cursor_visible = false,
		show_line_numbers = false,
		wrap_mode = Gtk.WrapMode.WORD,
		hexpand = true,
		vexpand = false,
		can_focus = false,
		focus_on_click = false,
		css_classes = { "code-editor" }
	};
	this.source_view.set_buffer(source_buffer);
	this.source_buffer = source_buffer;
	this.source_buffer.implicit_trailing_newline = false;
	this.source_view.pixels_below_lines = 0;
	this.source_view.add_css_class("code-editor");

	this.scrolled_window = new Gtk.ScrolledWindow() {
		hexpand = true,
		vexpand = true,
		margin_start = 2
	};
	this.scrolled_window.set_policy(Gtk.PolicyType.NEVER, Gtk.PolicyType.AUTOMATIC);

	this.rendered_box = new Gtk.Box(Gtk.Orientation.VERTICAL, 0) {
		hexpand = true,
		vexpand = false
	};
	this.rendered_box.add_css_class("oc-nested-markdown-content");
	this.stack = new Gtk.Stack() { hexpand = true, vexpand = false };
	this.stack.add_named(this.rendered_box, "rendered");
	this.source_scrolled = new Gtk.ScrolledWindow() {
		hexpand = true,
		vexpand = true,
		margin_start = 2
	};
	this.source_scrolled.set_policy(Gtk.PolicyType.NEVER, Gtk.PolicyType.AUTOMATIC);
	this.source_scrolled.set_child(this.source_view);
	this.stack.add_named(this.source_scrolled, "source");

	this.source_view.hexpand = true;
	this.source_view.vexpand = false;
	this.scrolled_window.set_child(this.stack);

	if (this.code_language == "markdown") {
		this.stack.visible_child_name = "rendered";
		this.nested_markdown_render = new MarkdownGtk.Render(this.rendered_box);
		this.nested_markdown_render.is_streaming = this.has_content;
		this.nested_markdown_render.start();
	} else {
		this.stack.visible_child_name = "source";
	}

	this.body_revealer = new Gtk.Revealer() {
		reveal_child = !this.has_collapsed_style,
		hexpand = true,
		vexpand = false,
		transition_type = Gtk.RevealerTransitionType.SLIDE_DOWN,
		transition_duration = 250
	};
	this.body_revealer.set_child(this.scrolled_window);
	this.container_box.append(this.body_revealer);

	this.source_view_realize_handler = this.source_view.realize.connect(() => {
		this.source_view.disconnect(this.source_view_realize_handler);
		this.source_view_realize_handler = 0;
		GLib.Idle.add(() => {
			if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
				this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.source_view, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
			}
			return false;
		});
	});
	this.rendered_box_realize_handler = this.rendered_box.realize.connect(() => {
		this.rendered_box.disconnect(this.rendered_box_realize_handler);
		this.rendered_box_realize_handler = 0;
		GLib.Idle.add(() => {
			if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
				this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
			}
			return false;
		});
	});

	this.has_widgets = true;
}
```

**ℹ️** Lifted from § **4** **Remove** (GtkSource, scrolled windows, stack, **`rendered_box`**, nested **`MarkdownGtk.Render`** + **`start()`**, **`body_revealer`**). **Not** **`view_source_toggle`** (§ **4** **Keep**). **Realize** hooks live here, not in the ctor (§ **6a.6**). **Order:** **`body_revealer.set_child(this.scrolled_window)`** then **`this.container_box.append(this.body_revealer)`** — **`this.container_box`** is created in the ctor (**§ 4a**); on the **lazy** path the ctor does **not** call **`body_widgets()`**, so the first append of **`body_revealer`** happens here when **`body_widgets()`** runs (handler or lazy **`end_code_block`** path). On the **eager** path, **`body_widgets()`** runs from the ctor before **`view_source_toggle`** (§ **4**), so the body is attached before the toggle **`clicked`** handlers reference **`stack`**.

### 5b. `fill_widgets()` — **✅**

#### Add

```vala
private void fill_widgets()
{
	Gtk.TextIter start_iter;
	Gtk.TextIter end_iter;
	this.source_buffer.get_bounds(out start_iter, out end_iter);
	this.source_buffer.delete(ref start_iter, ref end_iter);
	this.source_buffer.get_end_iter(out end_iter);
	this.source_buffer.insert(ref end_iter, this.code_text, -1);

	if (this.code_language == "markdown") {
		this.nested_markdown_render.add(this.code_text);
	}

	var widget_to_measure = (this.code_language == "markdown")
		? (Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box
		: (Gtk.Widget) this.source_view;
	GLib.Idle.add(() => {
		return this.resize_widget_callback(widget_to_measure, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
	});
}
```

**✅** **Precondition:** only called immediately after **`body_widgets(…)`** (lazy expand / bulk **`end_code_block`**) — **`source_buffer`** and nested renderer (when markdown) exist. **No** **`has_widgets` / null** guards — misuse is a bug.

**ℹ️** **Not** this method’s job to show the frame body (**`body_revealer`**). Callers own **reveal**; **`fill_widgets()`** only syncs **`code_text`** → GtkSource / nested **`add`** and queues **INITIAL** resize — **no** **`reveal_child`** branch.

**ℹ️** Use **`code_language == "markdown"`** for the nested branch (matches **`body_widgets()`**). **`nested_markdown_render != null`** is for **teardown** / **`flush`** in **`end_code_block`**, not for routine fill routing.

**ℹ️** Empty **`code_text`** still clears the buffer and **`add`s** empty to nested if the API allows; otherwise skip nested **`add`** when empty (implementation detail).

**ℹ️** Duplicate full-string nested **`add`** after incremental streaming — avoid by **call-site** discipline (**`fill_widgets()`** only on lazy/bulk paths) or compare lengths before bulk **`add`** (§ **6.1**).

### 6. Handlers / feed paths — **✅**

**✅** **Ctor-time `connect`s** — § **6a**. **Feed methods** — verbatim **§ 6.1** / **§ 6.2** below.

**✅** **`!has_widgets`** in **`clicked`** — **`body_widgets(true); fill_widgets()`** before touching **`body_revealer`** / **`stack`** when lazy policy requires it.

**✅** Likely **`clicked` / `realize`**: **`collapse_toggle_button`**, **`copy_button`**, **`full_view_toggle`**, **`new_chat_button`**, **`view_source_toggle`**, **`source_view` / `rendered_box`** — § **6a**.

---

#### 6.1 `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — `add_code_text` — **`code_text`** replaces **`code_content`** — **✅**

**✅** **`has_content`** is the **only** flag for per-chunk **`code_text`** accumulation: when **`!has_content`**, **`this.code_text += text`**, **return** — **no** **`code_block_content_updated()`** (nothing visible changed — § **💩** below). When **`has_content`** is **true**, **`add_code_text`** **only** runs the GtkSource / nested path — **contract:** **`has_widgets`** is already **true** (**`body_widgets()`** before any feed that routes into widgets — § **Lazy body**). **Do not** add **`if (!this.has_widgets) return;`** here: it duplicates **`has_content`** in plain language and hides bugs. **`assert(this.has_widgets)`** in debug builds if you want a hard check. **`code_text`** is reconciled from GtkSource **at `end_code_block`** when **`has_widgets`** (§ **6.2**). **Do not** read **`this.renderer.is_streaming`** in **`add_code_text`** / **`end_code_block`**.

**💩** **`Render.code_block_content_updated`** — **`ChatView`** connects **`scroll_to_bottom()`** only (`libollmchatgtk/ChatView.vala`). Emit when the **visible** chat should follow new content. **Off-tree** **`code_text +=`** only: **omit** — no GtkSource / nested update, no parent scroll needed. After **`insert` / nested `add`** with **`body_revealer.reveal_child`**: emit **before** inner **`Idle.add(scroll_bottom)`** (parent autoscroll). When **`!reveal_child`** (body hidden): **omit** — buffer grew but frame is collapsed; **same** as **`docs/plans/done/6.9-DONE-debugging-performance.md`** **experiment 1** (optional profiling toggle on both legacy call sites).

##### Remove

```vala
		public void add_code_text(string text)
		{
			// Accumulate content
			this.code_content.append(text);
			
			// Add to source buffer if it exists
			// GtkSource insert was profiled with this block commented out — not the dominant cost (issue elsewhere).
			if (this.source_buffer != null) {
				Gtk.TextIter end_iter;
				this.source_buffer.get_end_iter(out end_iter);
				this.source_buffer.insert(ref end_iter, text, -1);
			}
			
			// Stream to nested markdown renderer when this is a ```markdown block
			// Feeds libocmarkdown Parser.add (see docs/plans/done/6.8-DONE-fixing-large-restore.md); incremental line-by-line add is the dominant cost for large thinking blocks.
			if (this.nested_markdown_render != null) {
				// GLib.debug(
				// 	"nested add cum=%d chunk=%d",
				// 	(int) this.code_content.len,
				// 	(int) text.length
				// );
				this.nested_markdown_render.add(text);
			}
			
			// When collapsed, skip resize and scroll (body is hidden)
			if (!this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
				this.renderer.code_block_content_updated();
				return;
			}
			// Scroll visible content (rendered or source) to bottom after content is added
			GLib.Idle.add(() => {
				this.scroll_bottom();
				return false;
			});
			this.renderer.code_block_content_updated();
			// When content grows (e.g. newline), resize the frame so it expands with streamed content
			if (text.contains("\n")) {
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					if (!this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						return false;
					}
					var widget_to_resize = (this.nested_markdown_render != null)
						? (Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box
						: (Gtk.Widget) this.source_view;
					return this.resize_widget_callback(widget_to_resize, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
				});
			}
		}
```

##### Replace with

```vala
		public void add_code_text(string text)
		{
			if (!this.has_content) {
				this.code_text += text;
				return;
			}

			Gtk.TextIter end_iter;
			this.source_buffer.get_end_iter(out end_iter);
			this.source_buffer.insert(ref end_iter, text, -1);

			if (this.code_language == "markdown") {
				this.nested_markdown_render.add(text);
			}

			if (this.body_revealer != null && !this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
				return;
			}
			GLib.Idle.add(() => {
				this.scroll_bottom();
				return false;
			});
			this.renderer.code_block_content_updated();
			if (text.contains("\n")) {
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					if (this.body_revealer != null && !this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						return false;
					}
					var widget_to_resize = (this.code_language == "markdown")
						? (Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box
						: (Gtk.Widget) this.source_view;
					return this.resize_widget_callback(widget_to_resize, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
				});
			}
		}
```

**ℹ️** **`body_revealer`** — on this path **`has_widgets`** is **true**, so revealer exists (**`body_widgets()`**); **`!= null`** guard is for readability / future refactors. **`nested_markdown_render`** exists for **`markdown`** fences when **`has_widgets`**.

---

#### 6.2 `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — `end_code_block` — snapshot, finalize **`code_text`** — **✅**

**✅** **`has_widgets` / `has_content`** exist so **`end_code_block`** does **not** scatter **`source_buffer != null`**, **`source_view != null`**, **`nested_markdown_render`**, **`body_revealer`** checks for the **same** meaning everywhere — **embed built** vs **not**. **First** branch **`!has_widgets`**: **`code_text`** is already complete from **`add_code_text`** (**`!has_content`** path) — **no** buffer, **no** trim — **`code_block_ended`**, **return**. **Only then** the **`has_widgets`** path touches GtkSource (trim, snapshot into **`code_text`**, **`code_block_ended`**, nested **`flush`**, resize **`Idle`s`, **`code_block_content_updated`**, **`collapse_on_done`**).

**ℹ️** **`code_text` at close (order matters) — do not clear after `code_block_ended`:**
- **`code_text`** is the **authoritative fence-body string** for the **lifetime** of this **`RenderSourceView`** — **`new_chat_button`** (**§ Outline**, § **6a.4**), lazy **`fill_widgets()`**, and any path that must match GtkSource **without** reading **`source_buffer`**. Clearing it at **`end_code_block`** would wipe the body **after** the fence is done but **while** the widget still exists — **wrong**. A **new** fence is a **new** **`RenderSourceView`** (**`libocmarkdowngtk/Render.vala`** — **`new RenderSourceView(…)`**); the ctor sets **`code_text = ""`**. **Omit** today’s **`this.code_content = new StringBuilder()`** tail in **`Remove`**: that only reset a **duplicate** accumulator while **`new_chat`** still read **`source_buffer`** — **do not** reintroduce that as **`this.code_text = ""`** here.
- **`!has_widgets`** — **handled first** — fence never built GtkSource / stack; **`add_code_text`** only did **`code_text +=`**. **`code_text`** is already the **full** body — **no** buffer read, **no** trim — pass **`content`** to **`code_block_ended`**, **return**; **`code_text`** **unchanged** after notify.
- **`has_widgets`** — **`code_text`** is **reconciled from GtkSource** on this path: trim trailing newlines in place, **`get_bounds` → `get_text`** into **`this.code_text`** (canonical match for **`fill_widgets()`** / **`new_chat`**), then **`code_block_ended`**, then teardown; **`code_text`** **keeps** the final snapshot.

##### Remove

```vala
		public void end_code_block()
		{
			// Notify renderer with content and language
			var content = this.code_content.str;
			this.renderer.code_block_ended(content, this.code_language);

			// Remove trailing newline(s) from SourceView in place (no full buffer rewrite) to avoid extra blank line / scrollbar
			Gtk.TextIter end_iter;
			this.source_buffer.get_end_iter(out end_iter);
			var start_iter = end_iter;
			start_iter.backward_char();
			while (start_iter.get_char() == '\n' && !start_iter.is_start()) {
				start_iter.backward_char();
			}
			var del_start = start_iter;
			if (del_start.get_char() != '\n') {
				del_start.forward_char();
			}
			if (!del_start.equal(end_iter)) {
				this.source_buffer.delete(ref del_start, ref end_iter);
			}

			// Phase 2: when markdown block, flush the streamed nested renderer then resize and scroll to bottom
			if (this.nested_markdown_render != null) {
				// GLib.debug("nested markdown block flush len=%d", content.length);
				this.nested_markdown_render.flush();
				this.nested_markdown_render = null;
				GLib.Timeout.add(200, () => {
					if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
						this.scroll_bottom(this.scrolled_window);
					}
					return false;
				});
			}
			// Finalize the sourceview - resize based on content rules
			if (this.source_view != null) {
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					if (!this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						return false;
					}
					var result = this.resize_widget_callback(this.source_view, ResizeMode.FINAL);
					this.scroll_bottom(this.source_scrolled);
					return result;
				});
			}
			
			// Emit signal to notify that code block ended (for scrolling after final resize)
			// Use Idle to delay until after resize callback completes
			GLib.Idle.add(() => {
				this.renderer.code_block_content_updated();
				return false;
			});
			
			if (this.collapse_on_done) {
				GLib.Timeout.add(350, () => {
					this.body_revealer.reveal_child = false;
					this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-next-symbolic";
					this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Expand";
					this.view_source_toggle.visible = false;
					this.copy_button.visible = false;
					return false;
				});
			}
			
			// Clean up (keep code_language for header toggles on this widget)
			this.code_content = new StringBuilder();
		}
```

##### Replace with

```vala
		public void end_code_block()
		{
			if (!this.has_widgets) {
				var content = this.code_text;
				this.renderer.code_block_ended(content, this.code_language);
				return;
			}

			Gtk.TextIter end_iter;
			this.source_buffer.get_end_iter(out end_iter);
			var start_iter = end_iter;
			start_iter.backward_char();
			while (start_iter.get_char() == '\n' && !start_iter.is_start()) {
				start_iter.backward_char();
			}
			var del_start = start_iter;
			if (del_start.get_char() != '\n') {
				del_start.forward_char();
			}
			if (!del_start.equal(end_iter)) {
				this.source_buffer.delete(ref del_start, ref end_iter);
			}
			Gtk.TextIter s_iter;
			Gtk.TextIter e_iter;
			this.source_buffer.get_bounds(out s_iter, out e_iter);
			this.code_text = this.source_buffer.get_text(s_iter, e_iter, false);

			var content = this.code_text;
			this.renderer.code_block_ended(content, this.code_language);

			if (this.nested_markdown_render != null) {
				this.nested_markdown_render.flush();
				this.nested_markdown_render = null;
				GLib.Timeout.add(200, () => {
					if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
						this.scroll_bottom(this.scrolled_window);
					}
					return false;
				});
			}
			GLib.Idle.add(() => {
				if (!this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
					return false;
				}
				var result = this.resize_widget_callback(this.source_view, ResizeMode.FINAL);
				this.scroll_bottom(this.source_scrolled);
				return result;
			});

			GLib.Idle.add(() => {
				this.renderer.code_block_content_updated();
				return false;
			});

			if (this.collapse_on_done) {
				GLib.Timeout.add(350, () => {
					this.body_revealer.reveal_child = false;
					this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-next-symbolic";
					this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Expand";
					this.view_source_toggle.visible = false;
					this.copy_button.visible = false;
					return false;
				});
			}
		}
```

**ℹ️** **`has_widgets`** alone gates GtkSource — **`source_buffer`**, **`source_view`**, **`body_revealer`**, **`nested_markdown_render`** exist; **no** redundant **`!= null`** on those for the widget path (**misuse** = bug). **`!has_widgets`** path: **`code_text`** came only from **`add_code_text`** **`+=`** while **`!has_content`** (§ **6.1**). **`code_text`** is **not** cleared here — see bullets above.

### 6a. `libocmarkdowngtk/RenderSourceView.vala` — ctor signals (**one subsection per handler**) — **✅**

**ℹ️** **`Keep`** = today’s tree for review. **`Replace with`** only when it **differs** from **`Keep`** — if the implementation is unchanged, **omit** **`Replace with`** (no duplicate fences).

**✅** **Lazy embed:** On **`!has_widgets`**, **`body_revealer`**, **`stack`**, **`source_view`**, **`rendered_box`**, etc. are **not** built yet — **do not** read **`body_revealer.reveal_child`** or touch **`stack`** until **`body_widgets()`** has run (**`has_widgets`** true). The old tree assumed the embed always existed in the ctor; that is **wrong** for lazy policy.

**ℹ️** **Handler rule:** Any **`clicked`** path that calls **`body_widgets(…)`** calls **`fill_widgets()`** **right after**. Controls **hidden while the `.collapsed` frame body is collapsed** (**`copy_button`**, **`view_source_toggle`**, **`full_view_toggle`** — § Outline) **do not** put **`body_widgets(…)`** / **`fill_widgets()`** in their handlers — expand already did that. **`new_chat_button`** passes **`this.code_text`** — § Outline **`new_chat_button`** — **no** pairing in **`clicked`**.

**ℹ️** **Not every handler calls `body_widgets(…)`** — § Outline **Frame chrome naming** — **visibility-gated** buttons do not need **`body_widgets(…)`** when the click is impossible before the expand path already built and filled the body; **`collapse_toggle_button`** **always** runs **`body_widgets(true); fill_widgets();`** on lazy expand before reveal.

**ℹ️** Two toggles — § Outline **Frame chrome naming** — **`collapse_toggle_button`** = frame body; **`full_view_toggle`** = short vs full **body height** (rename from **`expand_button`**).

---

#### 6a.1 `collapse_toggle_button` — `clicked`

**✅** Touches **`body_revealer`**, **`stack`**, **`view_source_toggle`**, **`copy_button`**. On lazy path **`body_revealer` is absent until `body_widgets()`** — **`!has_widgets` → `body_widgets(true); fill_widgets();`** then continue (reveal / resize).

##### Keep

*Today — valid only when embed was always created in ctor.*

```vala
			this.collapse_toggle_button.clicked.connect(() => {
				if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
					this.body_revealer.reveal_child = false;
					this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-next-symbolic";
					this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Expand";
					// Hide view source and copy when collapsed
					this.view_source_toggle.visible = false;
					this.copy_button.visible = false;
					return;
				}
				// Reveal first so body is visible; then idle-add resize so widget can be realized
				this.body_revealer.reveal_child = true;
				this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-up-symbolic";
				this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Collapse";
				this.view_source_toggle.visible = (this.code_language == "markdown");
				this.copy_button.visible = true;
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					// Use the stack's visible child so we measure the widget that will be shown (and realized)
					Gtk.Widget widget = this.stack.visible_child;
					if (widget == null) {
						widget = (this.code_language == "markdown")
							? (Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box
							: (Gtk.Widget) this.source_view;
					}
					return this.resize_widget_callback(widget, ResizeMode.REVEAL_BODY);
				});
			});
```

##### Replace with

```vala
			this.collapse_toggle_button.clicked.connect(() => {
				if (!this.has_widgets) {
					this.body_widgets(true);
					this.fill_widgets();
				}
				if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
					this.body_revealer.reveal_child = false;
					this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-next-symbolic";
					this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Expand";
					this.view_source_toggle.visible = false;
					this.copy_button.visible = false;
					return;
				}
				this.body_revealer.reveal_child = true;
				this.collapse_toggle_button.icon_name = "go-up-symbolic";
				this.collapse_toggle_button.tooltip_text = "Collapse";
				this.view_source_toggle.visible = (this.code_language == "markdown");
				this.copy_button.visible = true;
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					Gtk.Widget widget = this.stack.visible_child;
					if (widget == null) {
						widget = (this.code_language == "markdown")
							? (Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box
							: (Gtk.Widget) this.source_view;
					}
					return this.resize_widget_callback(widget, ResizeMode.REVEAL_BODY);
				});
			});
```

#### 6a.2 `copy_button` — `clicked`

**ℹ️** **`.collapsed`** — **`copy_button`** is **only shown when the body is expanded**; collapse **hides** it — the handler does **not** call **`body_widgets()`** / **`fill_widgets()`**. **Visible copy while the body is collapsed** — **bug**.

*Plan implementation matches **Keep** — no **Replace with** (expand path + **`§4`** lazy matrix already give **`has_widgets`** before copy is clickable for **`.collapsed`** chrome).*

#### 6a.3 `full_view_toggle` — `clicked` *(rename from `expand_button`)*

**ℹ️** **Ctor** — field, **`button_box.append`**, and **`clicked`** — **§ 4b** (authoritative fenced Vala). **§ 6a.7** — **`resize_widget_callback`** show/hide branch.

##### Keep

*Today’s names.*

```vala
			expand_button.clicked.connect(() => {
				is_expanded = !is_expanded;
				if (is_expanded) {
					expand_button.icon_name = "pan-up-symbolic";
					expand_button.tooltip_text = "Collapse";
					GLib.Idle.add(() => {

					});

```

#### 6a.6 `source_view` / `rendered_box` — `realize`

##### Keep

*Ctor-time connect — invalid if widgets do not exist yet (lazy path).*

```vala
			this.source_view_realize_handler = this.source_view.realize.connect(() => {
				this.source_view.disconnect(this.source_view_realize_handler);
				this.source_view_realize_handler = 0;
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.source_view, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
					}
					return false;
				});
			});
			this.rendered_box_realize_handler = this.rendered_box.realize.connect(() => {
				this.rendered_box.disconnect(this.rendered_box_realize_handler);
				this.rendered_box_realize_handler = 0;
				GLib.Idle.add(() => {
					if (this.body_revealer.reveal_child) {
						this.resize_widget_callback((Gtk.Widget) this.rendered_box, ResizeMode.INITIAL);
					}
					return false;
				});
			});
```

##### Implementation *(not a Vala **Replace with** — structural move)*

**✅** **Remove** ctor-time **`realize.connect`** when **`source_view`** / **`rendered_box`** are created inside **`body_widgets()`**. **Add** the same lambda bodies at the **end** of **`body_widgets()`** immediately after each widget exists (or connect once per widget there). **`body_revealer`** is non-null by then; **`has_widgets`** becomes true after **`body_widgets()`** completes — idle resize matches today’s behaviour.

*(No fenced block here — pair with § **5a** **Add**.)*

#### 6a.7 `resize_widget_callback` — `expand_button` → `full_view_toggle` — **✅**

**Why:** Show/hide **inner** pan-down / pan-up when content overflows — same logic as today; **rename** only (**§ 3b**, § **4b**).

##### Remove
